title: Phase D: optional adoption UX improvements
outOfScope: mobile-stack-quality Xcode fixture (D-3 deferred; SPM-only documentation is sufficient)
特定プロジェクト向け preset

sources.reason: Phase D items D-1/D-2 and explicit D-3 deferral.

problemStatement: Phase D improves optional adoption UX: Cursor rules opt in rather than force Work Items on read-only tasks, and the installer warns about dirty worktrees and tracked hygiene files before --create-adoption fails. D-3 Xcode CI fixture remains deferred after Phase A SPM scope documentation.

riskAssessment.reason: alwaysApply default change affects every new installation; preflight warnings must not weaken fail-closed adoption checks.

executionDecision.reason: D-1 and D-2 are specified; D-3 explicitly deferred with documented rationale.

preReviewWarnings: Confirm alwaysApply false still allows manual rule enablement in Cursor.
Confirm adoption preflight still fails closed after warnings.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Installer and Cursor defaults affect all new adoptions.

acceptance: Installed Cursor rule defaults to alwaysApply: false with documentation for opt-in enforcement.
docs/reference/upgrade.md notes the Cursor rule default change for adopters upgrading or reinstalling.
scripts/install_ai_cockpit.py emits WARN messages for dirty worktrees and tracked hygiene files before --create-adoption hard failure.
Adoption preflight still requires clean worktree, initial commit, and no active Work Item.
D-3 mobile-stack-quality Xcode fixture is not added; deferral recorded in Summary.
tests cover preflight warnings and installed Cursor rule default.
guidelines: Do not weaken --create-adoption fail-closed checks; warnings only.
Document D-3 deferral explicitly in knownGaps or overclaimPrevention.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Phase D scopes Cursor rule and adoption doc updates for alwaysApply and preflight guidance.

rollbackNote: Revert alwaysApply, installer preflight warnings, and Phase D documentation.
